ui.arfc.category.attitude_target.title: Attitude Toward Goals
ui.arfc.category.rythm_of_work.title: Work Rhythm
ui.arfc.category.function.title: Role Preference
ui.arfc.category.communication.title: Communication Style
ui.arfc.category.attitude_target.desc: What are your expectations for the final grade?
ui.arfc.category.rythm_of_work.desc: What kind of work pace do you prefer?
ui.arfc.category.function.desc: What type of role do you like to play in a team?
ui.arfc.category.communication.desc: What is your preferred communication style?
ui.home.project_desc: Quantify your collaboration style to help you find the best role in a team and improve cooperation efficiency and outcome quality.
ui.home.warning_1: Test results are for reference only. Please do not over-interpret them.
ui.home.warning_2: Successful teamwork requires continuous communication and adjustment. Real chemistry comes from actual interaction and understanding.
ui.home.purpose1.title: Quantify Expectations & Reduce Hidden Costs
ui.home.purpose1.desc: The biggest source of friction in group projects is differing expectations for grades. This test allows everyone to be transparent from the start about whether they aim for excellence or just a passing grade. Aligning goals early helps avoid emotional drain later.
ui.home.purpose2.title: Optimize Role Division & Boost Team Efficiency
ui.home.purpose2.desc: Many teams fail due to uneven or overlapping responsibilities. This test identifies each member's strengths and preferences (e.g., who excels at proofreading vs. presenting). It enables data-driven role assignment instead of relying on relationships or random allocation.
ui.home.purpose3.title: Adjust Communication Styles & Strengthen Team Chemistry
ui.home.purpose3.desc: Poor communication is a common team issue, especially under pressure. The test reveals each member's preferred style — direct vs. gentle, frequent updates vs. occasional syncs. Understanding these differences helps the team adapt and reduce misunderstandings.
ui.quiz.step1.title: Complete the Test
ui.quiz.step1.desc: Answer honestly. There are no right or wrong answers — just respond according to your actual habits and preferences!
ui.quiz.step2.title: View Your Results
ui.quiz.step2.desc: Discover how your unique personality type influences your behavior and collaboration style in a team.
ui.quiz.step3.title: Find Your Position
ui.quiz.step3.desc: Based on your test results, identify the most suitable team role for you and leverage your strengths!
ui.quiz.progress.title: Test Progress
ui.quiz.disclamer:
Note: This test is for reference only. The scoring has not been academically validated.
We do not collect any personal sensitive data.
All results are stored only in your browser's local cache.
ui.result.title: Your ARFC Role is...
ui.result.share_text:
My ARFC is %1, and my representative role is %2!
%3
%4

Here are my personality traits:
%5

I beg you, come test!
ui.about.title: About ARFC
ui.about.quote: Group projects are an inevitable part of college life; I'm just here to help you find the right teammates.
ui.about.story_p1: The story begins quite simply. On the afternoon of April 13th, I saw the ACGTI project created by tianxingleo in the KTFC group and thought the concept was brilliant.
ui.about.story_p2: That night, while chatting with my good friend WolfKnight, I shared the link with him. Our conversation eventually shifted to a recent class project. Although we submitted it on time, the process was draining. We didn't know each other well enough, leading to gaps in expectations regarding deadlines, time commitment, and even communication styles. I didn't want to be too blunt, but being subtle meant I wasn't being understood... it was a frustrating spot to be in.
ui.about.story_p3: So I thought: 'What if we could send each other a test before forming a group to see what "species" we are? Maybe that would save us from unnecessary friction.' And well, here we are—this absurd yet interesting idea was born.
ui.about.system_title: ARFC System: Four Dimensions Designed for Group Work
ui.about.system_p1: This system doesn't deal with abstract personality traits. Instead, we focus on where group projects usually go wrong: Grades, Deadlines, Division of Labor, and Communication. Most of the time, if just one of these areas is off, the whole collaboration becomes a mess.
ui.about.system_p2: If these four dimensions align, the 'suffering index' of your collaboration should drop significantly (I hope).
ui.about.dev_p1: To be honest, I'm an App developer. Web development isn't my forte, and I have some 'traumatic' memories with React. Much of this was generated with the help of AI. I’ll refactor the code once my FYP and internship are sorted out.
ui.about.dev_status_p1: This is primarily a 'just for fun' project. However, my biggest hope is that it can actually help students who feel frustrated with group work, helping them find compatible partners and making the whole experience smoother.
ui.about.join_p1: This is an experiment with LLM Agent capabilities. So far, it feels like AI can do the work of two people, but you have to be very precise with your prompts—otherwise, it might generate a bunch of nonsense.
ui.about.join_issue: Suggestions: Feel free to open an Issue to tell me if the quiz is accurate or if the questions need work.
ui.about.join_code: Code Optimization: I’ll handle this myself (awkward smile).
ui.about.join_trans: Translation Improvements: Again, Issues & PRs are welcome!
ui.about.join_share: Support: If you find this useful, or just want to see if your friend is a Capybara, why not share it with them?
question.1: If the teacher says this assignment won't be graded, I would just complete it casually.
question.2: I care more about how beautiful the PPT looks than the depth of the presentation content.
question.3: I cannot accept team members frequently missing classes.
question.4: When a teammate falls behind, I tend to understand their difficulties rather than blame their attitude.
question.5: I really hate it when people come to meetings or ask questions without any preparation.
question.6: I am willing to invest enough time in group discussions and work for the project.
question.7: Even if the chosen topic is not in my familiar area, I will still try my best to learn and complete the work.
question.8: I will proactively check formatting, word count, and other requirements multiple times, and seek advice from teammates and the teacher.
question.9: I prefer having clear written division of responsibilities rather than working based on assumptions.
question.10: I believe 'efficiency' is more important than 'consensus'. Sometimes we need to push toward a conclusion rather than trying to please everyone.
question.11: If the report gets criticized by the teacher, I would feel very frustrated and it might affect my motivation afterward.
question.12: I can accept completely changing the original design at the last minute if a better idea comes up.
question.13: After division of labor, I usually proactively upload the materials I will need to the shared document.
question.14: My efficiency in the last 24 hours before the deadline is at least twice as high as usual.
question.15: If someone urges me to submit a draft in the first week after grouping, I would feel very stressed.
question.16: I hope someone will remind me when certain parts need to be completed.
question.17: I dislike seeing a bunch of deadlines and prefer to finish everything as soon as possible.
question.18: I like to reserve 1-2 days as a buffer for revisions.
question.19: I often do homework or study at dawn rather than during the day.
question.20: Even if there isn't enough time left to polish the report, I will still do my best to improve it.
question.21: I like to make a schedule and complete work step by step.
question.22: When the deadline is approaching and we are far behind schedule, I can stay calm and reallocate tasks.
question.23: I would rather handle dozens of literature reviews by myself than give a 5-minute presentation.
question.24: I am good at spotting typos, awkward wording, or inconsistent formatting in reports.
question.25: During group discussions, I am usually the one who summarizes everyone's scattered ideas into clear conclusions.
question.26: I am good at assigning appropriate workloads to others based on their abilities.
question.27: I like to take the lead in helping teammates clearly understand what needs to be prepared.
question.28: I prefer that everyone supervises each other rather than electing one leader to handle coordination.
question.29: Rather than emailing the teacher myself to ask questions, I prefer someone else to do it.
question.30: I am good at generating creative ideas from scratch, but I find the detailed execution afterward boring.
question.31: I consider myself someone who values clear work boundaries. If it's not my responsibility, I won't get involved.
question.32: When the team is stuck in disagreement, I am usually the one who proposes the final compromise.
question.33: If I am unhappy with a teammate's performance, I would rather quietly finish that part myself than confront them directly.
question.34: I prefer holding meetings over group chats because it's hard to sense tone through text.
question.35: I think a celebration dinner after finishing the group project is essential.
question.36: When a teammate says they have completed their part, I will post a thank-you message in the group.
question.37: When making suggestions, I often use softening words like 'maybe' or 'perhaps'.
question.38: I try to avoid being too direct in order to prevent making others feel uncomfortable or embarrassed.
question.39: I usually spend several minutes thinking about the content and wording before sending a message.
question.40: I find it very awkward when the meeting suddenly goes silent.
question.41: I don't think we need to put in a lot of enthusiasm toward teammates since we're working together for the first time.
question.42: I believe effective communication means adjusting to the other person's state rather than just saying what I want to say.
question.43: Rather than expressing my own ideas, I prefer to observe everyone's intentions and then show support.
role.lion.tag.name: Excellence Navigator
role.lion.short: You are the commander in the storm — wherever your flag points, that is the destination.
role.lion.desc: You come with a high-output, strong-control leader profile. You cannot tolerate chaos or inefficiency and will proactively take on the heaviest responsibilities to keep the team precisely on track.
role.lion.talent_desc: You have a powerful sense of direction and can quickly break down complex tasks and assign them effectively, getting the team into battle mode from day one.
role.lion.pitfall_desc: In pursuit of efficiency, you sometimes overlook teammates' sense of participation, which can turn communication into one-way instructions.
role.lion.self_intro: I will proactively take responsibility to ensure we stay precisely on track. My strong execution allows us to break down complex tasks quickly and get into battle mode from day one!
role.swan.tag.name: Graceful Coordinator
role.swan.short: On the path to perfection, you gently hold everyone's hand.
role.swan.desc: The 'elegant hub' of the team. Your pursuit of excellence is hidden behind a gentle tone. You excel at motivating everyone to go the extra mile without creating pressure.
role.swan.talent_desc: You are skilled at creating a positive collaborative atmosphere and balancing team goals with members' feelings, helping everyone achieve excellence in a comfortable environment.
role.swan.pitfall_desc: To maintain harmony, you may silently take on extra work when dealing with irresponsible teammates because you feel uncomfortable confronting them.
role.swan.self_intro: I am good at creating a positive collaborative atmosphere and can balance team goals with members' feelings, helping everyone achieve excellence comfortably.
role.dog.tag.name: Meticulous Practitioner
role.dog.short: While everyone else is making noise, you quietly check for leaks and fill in the gaps.
role.dog.desc: The classic 'logistics master'. You may not enjoy standing in the spotlight giving presentations, but your near-perfect formatting and accurate data are your most powerful statements.
role.dog.talent_desc: You have a natural sensitivity to details and can catch every overlooked logical flaw — the team's final quality insurance.
role.dog.pitfall_desc: When plans suddenly change or creativity runs wild, your excessive rigor may cause anxiety and hinder flexibility.
role.dog.self_intro: I excel at quietly ensuring quality and catching every detail others might miss.
role.cat.tag.name: Quiet Guardian
role.cat.short: In the early hours when the world is asleep, you are the light guarding every detail.
role.cat.desc: The classic 'planning-type night owl'. You don’t fight for speaking time, but in the unseen late nights, you turn messy drafts into polished, meaningful work.
role.cat.talent_desc: You produce high-quality output through quiet independent thinking and have a keen sense of your teammates’ needs — a low-profile but reliable teammate.
role.cat.pitfall_desc: Your quiet style may cause others to overlook your contributions. It’s recommended to proactively share your progress when appropriate.
role.cat.self_intro: I produce high-quality work through quiet, deep thinking and have a fine-tuned awareness of team needs. I’m a low-key but dependable teammate.
role.cheetah.tag.name: Agile Decision Maker
role.cheetah.short: The closer the deadline gets, the brighter your eyes become.
role.cheetah.desc: The team's 'deadline warrior'. You may seem casual during normal times, but in the final 24 hours, you switch into overdrive and lead the team to a last-minute comeback.
role.cheetah.talent_desc: In extreme time pressure and chaos, you can still calmly prioritize and make the best decisions for the project.
role.cheetah.pitfall_desc: Your low motivation in the early stages may make planners feel uneasy. Showing more engagement early on helps build trust.
role.cheetah.self_intro: In the chaos of extreme time pressure, I can still calmly prioritize and make the best decisions for the project.
role.dolphin.tag.name: Dynamic Empathizer
role.dolphin.short: With your optimistic energy, you smooth away all the anxiety before the deadline.
role.dolphin.desc: The 'inspiration remedy' of the team. When the atmosphere hits rock bottom, your presence always brings new possibilities. Your empathy helps mend a team on the verge of breaking.
role.dolphin.talent_desc: You excel at compromising and communicating under pressure, turning rigid discussions into flexible conversations where everyone feels supported.
role.dolphin.pitfall_desc: Because you care so much about everyone’s feelings, you may hesitate when a tough 'either-or' decision is needed.
role.dolphin.self_intro: I excel at communicating and compromising under pressure, turning stiff discussions into flexible dialogues where everyone feels supported.
role.eagle.tag.name: Visionary Pioneer
role.eagle.short: You don’t look at the footsteps on the ground — you look at the inspiration in the stars.
role.eagle.desc: The team's 'creativity engine'. You lack patience for tedious foundational work, but once you have an interesting idea, you can burn energy at the last moment and turn it into something stunning.
role.eagle.talent_desc: You can think outside the box and provide fresh angles that impress the teacher — the key to elevating an ordinary project.
role.eagle.pitfall_desc: After the creative spark, your enthusiasm tends to fade during the tedious execution phase. You need to force yourself to focus on formatting and rules.
role.eagle.self_intro: I excel at thinking outside the box and providing fresh perspectives that can turn an ordinary project into something outstanding.
role.owl.tag.name: Deep Thinker
role.owl.short: In the lonely late night, you are building the deepest soul for the team.
role.owl.desc: The solitary 'thought craftsman'. You prefer to delve into difficult literature alone at night, using your intellectual depth to support the entire project’s logical framework.
role.owl.talent_desc: You have high academic discipline and research spirit, producing insightful content that provides a solid foundation for the project.
role.owl.pitfall_desc: Being too immersed in your own thinking world may cause you to fall out of sync with the team. Increasing communication frequency is recommended.
role.owl.self_intro: I have strong academic discipline and research ability, producing insightful content that gives the project a solid logical foundation.
role.wolf.tag.name: Pragmatic Manager
role.wolf.short: You don’t care about extra decoration — you only care about hitting the target on time.
role.wolf.desc: The 'cost-performance' gatekeeper. You understand efficiency well and can precisely divide tasks, ensuring the team passes safely without wasting extra effort.
role.wolf.talent_desc: You are very clear about responsibility division and can effectively prevent scope creep, allowing everyone to complete tasks with minimal wasted effort.
role.wolf.pitfall_desc: When facing sudden 'pursuit of excellence' demands, you may appear inflexible because you stick to the original boundaries.
role.wolf.self_intro: I keep responsibilities clearly defined and prevent scope creep, ensuring everyone can complete tasks efficiently.
role.golden_retriever.tag.name: Friendly Stabilizer
role.golden_retriever.short: As long as everyone can pass smoothly, you are the warmest background support.
role.golden_retriever.desc: The 'atmosphere supporter' of the team. You never fight for leadership, but whenever support or legwork is needed, you are always the one who smiles and says 'sure'.
role.golden_retriever.talent_desc: You have extremely high adaptability and cooperation ability, fitting into any team style and serving as an indispensable force for maintaining group harmony.
role.golden_retriever.pitfall_desc: When professional opinions are needed, you may hide your own thoughts to accommodate others, causing valuable suggestions to be overlooked.
role.golden_retriever.self_intro: I have high adaptability and can fit into any team. I’m the reliable force that helps maintain harmony within the group.
role.elephant.tag.name: Steady Executor
role.elephant.short: Every step you take is to make the team move more steadily.
role.elephant.desc: The embodiment of 'rules and execution'. You believe in the power of clear documentation. As long as the task list is clear, you will complete everything methodically during the day, giving the team the most solid sense of security.
role.elephant.talent_desc: You reliably complete all assigned tasks and have natural respect for formats and processes — the most stable productivity in the team.
role.elephant.pitfall_desc: When plans are suddenly overturned or a sprint is required, you may feel uncomfortable due to the disrupted rhythm. Practicing flexibility with uncertainty is helpful.
role.elephant.self_intro: I reliably complete all assigned tasks and respect formats and processes, providing the team with the most stable output.
role.capybara.tag.name: Chill Collaborator
role.capybara.short: In the midst of heated discussion, you are the most peaceful presence.
role.capybara.desc: The team's 'pressure relief valve'. No matter how close the deadline or how intense the arguments get, you remain calm and provide necessary support in the most laid-back way.
role.capybara.talent_desc: You have amazing stress resistance and tolerance, able to absorb the team’s negative emotions and complete support work without adding any pressure.
role.capybara.pitfall_desc: Being too easygoing may be seen as lacking initiative. Showing clearer enthusiasm at key moments is recommended.
role.capybara.self_intro: I have strong emotional resilience and can absorb negativity while providing calm support without adding pressure.
role.raccoon.tag.name: Clever Guide
role.raccoon.short: You always find the most efficient path that still leads to the goal.
role.raccoon.desc: The team's 'path scout'. Faced with complicated work, you don’t brute-force it — you look for tools, templates, or shortcuts to solve problems in the smartest way.
role.raccoon.talent_desc: You excel at finding existing resources and tools to boost team efficiency, producing reasonable results with minimal effort in limited time.
role.raccoon.pitfall_desc: Because you pursue speed and cleverness, you may sometimes overlook depth or rigor. Having detail-oriented teammates to double-check helps.
role.raccoon.self_intro: I’m good at finding resources and tools to improve efficiency and deliver solid results with minimal wasted effort.
role.fox.tag.name: Smooth Creative
role.fox.short: You turn a serious discussion into an enjoyable social experience.
role.fox.desc: The 'flexible lubricant'. You are skilled at using humor to resolve conflicts. Rather than focusing only on report content, you care more about how to impress the teacher through beautiful PPTs or presentations.
role.fox.talent_desc: You have strong presentation and social skills, able to package even dry work in a highly attractive way and usually score well in external presentations.
role.fox.pitfall_desc: After the creative part is done and repetitive detail work begins, you tend to lose focus. Using a strong task list to remind yourself helps.
role.fox.self_intro: I’m skilled at using humor to resolve differences and care about making our work visually and presentation-wise appealing to impress others.
role.sloth.tag.name: Minimalist Executor
role.sloth.short: You never waste any energy and only act at the most critical moments.
role.sloth.desc: The practitioner of 'minimalism'. You dislike long meetings and meaningless communication. You quietly stay in your position and only produce the minimum required result at the last moment.
role.sloth.talent_desc: You can instantly spot ineffective work and lock all energy onto the 'must-pass' indicators — the team’s energy-saving core.
role.sloth.pitfall_desc: Too little interaction may give teammates the impression that you don’t care. Increasing reply frequency at key moments is better.
role.sloth.self_intro: I can spot ineffective tasks at a glance and focus energy only on what is truly necessary to pass — the team’s efficiency core.
role.panda.tag.name: Easygoing Supporter
role.panda.short: You go with the flow, yet always quietly fill in the gaps when needed.
role.panda.desc: The classic 'free-spirited teammate'. You don’t like being bound by schedules, but whenever someone in the group asks for help, you often quietly provide completed materials in the middle of the night.
role.panda.talent_desc: You have extremely high adaptability and can adjust your role anytime according to the team’s needs — the best person to fill team weaknesses.
role.panda.pitfall_desc: Your casual rhythm may frustrate planners. Giving more concrete 'delivery promises' increases team trust.
role.panda.self_intro: I have high flexibility and can adapt my role to fill whatever gaps the team has at any time.
